title: Epic 1: Project Scaffolding & Shared Foundations
description: Establish the monorepo structure and core utilities required for cross-package synchronization. This provides the 'bones' of the system. Includes PNPM workspace initialization, tsup configuration, and the shared monotonic clock utility.

title: Story 1.1: Initialize PNPM Monorepo & Build Tooling
description:
Establish the base monorepo structure.

Background: A monorepo is essential for decoupling the core media engine from framework-specific integrations while maintaining type safety across the entire project.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Root package.json and pnpm-workspace.yaml created.
- Directory structure: packages/web2hls, packages/react, apps/example-tanstack.
- tsup configured for dual ESM/CJS in library packages.
- Shared tsconfig.base.json and .prettierrc established.

title: Story 1.2: Implement Shared Monotonic Clock Utility
description:
Create a high-resolution monotonic clock for A/V synchronization.

Background: Accurate timestamping is the foundation of media streaming. Drift between video and audio is the most common cause of poor stream quality.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Package 'shared-clock' created or utility in utils/clock.ts.
- Uses performance.now() for microsecond precision.
- Supports an origin time shared across Main Thread and Web Workers.
- Provides consistent timestamps for both VideoFrame and AudioData.

title: Story 1.3: Define Framework-Agnostic Core API Types
description:
Establish the shared type system for the pipeline.

Background: The PipelineEvent protocol ensures that the Main Thread and Web Workers can communicate predictably using structured messages.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Define PipelineEvent interface.
- Define StreamingConfig and Telemetry types.
- Export types for use in both web2hls and @web2hls/react.

title: Epic 5: YouTube Platform Integration & Auth
description: Implement the OAuth2 PKCE flow and the YouTube Live API client to manage broadcasts and streams securely in a pure client-side environment.

title: Story 5.1: Implement OAuth2 PKCE Flow & Token Management
description:
Secure client-side YouTube authentication.

Background: PKCE is the security standard for browser-based OAuth2. Storing tokens in sessionStorage ensures they are cleared when the tab is closed.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Implement OAuth2 PKCE flow.
- abstract TokenStorage interface for extensibility.
- Default to sessionStorage for access/refresh tokens.
- Request minimal scopes (youtube.upload, youtube.readonly).

title: Story 5.2: Implement YouTube Live Resource Client
description:
Manage YouTube Live resources via API.

Background: Automating stream creation removes the need for the user to navigate the YouTube Studio dashboard manually.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Create 'liveBroadcast' and 'liveStream' resources.
- Bind broadcast to stream.
- Handle YouTube API quota limits gracefully.

title: Epic 7: React 19 Integration & Developer Experience
description: Expose the pipeline through modern React 19 primitives, including hooks, actions, and telemetry synchronization (useSyncExternalStore).

title: Epic 4: MPEG-TS Muxing & HLS Segmenting
description: Mux the encoded bitstreams into MPEG-TS packets and organize them into HLS-compliant segments using the Mediabunny library wrapper within the worker.

title: Story 4.1: Integrate Mediabunny Muxer
description:
Wrap Mediabunny for MPEG-TS packetization.

Background: MPEG-TS is the required container for HLS. Mediabunny handles the complex interleaving of bitstreams.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Mediabunny integrated into the worker.
- Interleaves EncodedVideoChunk and EncodedAudioChunk.
- Outputs raw MPEG-TS packets.

title: Story 4.2: Implement PAT/PMT & Keyframe Management
description:
Ensure HLS-compliant segment boundaries.

Background: HLS players require segments to start with a keyframe and valid Program Association/Map Tables (PAT/PMT).

Acceptance Criteria:
- Forces Keyframe generation at segment boundaries.
- Prepends PAT/PMT metadata to every new segment.
- Validates bitstream compliance.

title: Story 4.3: Implement HLS Segmenter
description:
Buffer packets into discrete HLS segments.

Background: Segments (.ts files) are the units of delivery in HLS. Configurable duration (e.g., 2s) balances latency and stability.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Buffers MPEG-TS packets based on time duration.
- Finalizes segments and passes them to the uploader queue.
- Supports configurable segment duration.

title: Epic 3: High-Performance WebCodecs Encoding
description: Integrate the WebCodecs API within a dedicated Web Worker to transform raw frames into H.264/AAC bitstreams. This ensures high-performance encoding without blocking the UI thread and includes fallback profile logic.

title: Story 3.1: Implement Web Worker Encoder Orchestrator
description:
Setup the encoding Web Worker.

Background: Media encoding is CPU-intensive. Moving it to a Web Worker prevents UI jank and ensures a smooth 60fps experience for the user.

Acceptance Criteria:
- encoder.worker.ts implementation.
- Establishes MessageChannel for low-latency communication.
- Handles deserialization of VideoFrame/AudioData objects.

title: Story 3.2: Implement VideoEncoder with H.264 Fallback
description:
Wrap WebCodecs VideoEncoder with fallback logic.

Background: H.264 profile support varies wildly across browsers (especially Safari). Automatic fallback ensures the stream starts even on restricted hardware.

Acceptance Criteria:
- VideoEncoder initialization with hardware acceleration preference.
- Implements 'High' -> 'Main' -> 'Baseline' fallback chain.
- Produces EncodedVideoChunk objects.

title: Story 3.3: Implement AudioEncoder (AAC) & Thermal Monitoring
description:
Wrap WebCodecs AudioEncoder and monitor system health.

Background: AAC is the standard for HLS. Thermal monitoring allows the pipeline to proactively reduce bitrate before the OS throttles the browser.

Acceptance Criteria:
- AudioEncoder implementation for AAC.
- Produces EncodedAudioChunk objects.
- Reports thermal 'critical' or 'fair' states via telemetry.

title: Story 3.4: Implement Aggressive Media Resource Disposal
description:
Enforce strict memory management in the worker.

Background: VideoFrame and AudioData are heavy resources. Failure to .close() them immediately leads to 'Out of Memory' crashes within minutes.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Explicit .close() calls on every processed or skipped frame/chunk.
- Memory leak validation using a 30-minute stress test.
- Stable heap size maintained within ±10%.

title: Epic 2: Media Capture & Engine Orchestration
description: Implement the raw media capture loops for Canvas and AudioContext, managed by the framework-agnostic core pipeline. This involves handling high-frequency capture loops and reporting telemetry for frame drops and latency.

title: Story 2.1: Implement Canvas Frame Capture Loop
description:
Develop the high-performance canvas capture loop.

Background: Capture must be fast enough to avoid jitter but smart enough to drop frames when the encoder is struggling.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Supports HTML5 Canvas and OffscreenCanvas.
- Produces VideoFrame objects at configurable FPS.
- Implements frame-dropping logic based on encoder backpressure.
- Reports real-time capture FPS and drop counts.

title: Story 2.2: Implement Web Audio Capture Loop
description:
Develop the synchronized audio capture loop.

Background: Synthesized audio (Web Audio API) provides higher fidelity than microphone capture and enables pixel-perfect sync with canvas animations.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Captures raw PCM data from an AudioContext.
- Produces AudioData objects.
- Uses the shared monotonic clock for timestamps.

title: Story 2.3: Implement Core StreamingPipeline Orchestrator
description:
Develop the framework-agnostic orchestrator.

Background: The orchestrator is the 'brain' of the system, managing the lifecycle of capture, encoding, and delivery.

Acceptance Criteria:
- StreamingPipeline class implementation.
- Exposes start(), stop(), and pause() methods.
- Aggregates telemetry from all sub-modules.
- Handles initialization of canvas and audio sources.

title: Epic 6: Reliable HLS Delivery (Uploader)
description: Implement the sequential HTTP PUT uploader with exponential backoff and retry logic to ensure reliable delivery to YouTube even with network fluctuations.
